_meta.source_data: data/raw/HA_Oloika.sqlite (June 2025, 8 plugs / 5 households, 176 valid cooking sessions)
_meta.session_definition: split on >20min gap; valid if duration>=2min OR approx_kwh>=0.02; see analysis/analyze_personas.py
_meta.green_window_baseline: 65% of cooking energy already lands in solar (green-light) windows; ~35% (26 kWh of 73.5) is shiftable
_meta.caveat: 5 self-selected e-cooker-trial households are NOT representative of the 84 customers / 5000 people; personas are illustrative. Restaurant persona is SYNTHETIC (no restaurant metered).

personas.label: Daytime-ready frequent cook
personas.role: Already cooks with the sun. Reinforce with rewards; little to move.

personas.label: Evening-anchored short cook (grid-stressing)
personas.role: PRIMARY TARGET. Cooks at the 20:00-22:00 peak on battery. Nudge earlier meals / pre-cook at midday.

personas.label: High-energy midday household
personas.role: Biggest household load but well-timed to solar. Protect the window; trim the evening tail.

personas.label: Small eatery / 'hotel' (electric-cooking scenario)
personas.assumption_basis: No restaurant was metered. Sized from an assumed electric appliance list, roughly 8x the heaviest trial household (B).
personas.flexibility: split: LOW at meal service, HIGH for bulk prep (beans, water heating)
personas.role: SYNTHETIC. Largest single-customer lever if electrified: shift bulk prep (beans/water) to midday solar; keep service-time cooking fixed.
